title: Add Pi as a supported harness

rationale: Pi supports OpenAI-compatible local backends through models.json custom providers rather than the Claude/Codex env-only paths.
An isolated PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR under the CCL state directory keeps the user's default ~/.pi/agent config untouched and makes rollback consistent with existing CCL state isolation.
Pi is wired as a first-class harness in detection, picker, helper aliases, verify, ccl run, documentation, and tests.
alternatives_considered: Reuse OPENAI_BASE_URL against Pi's built-in OpenAI provider; rejected because Pi documents custom endpoints via models.json.
Write directly to ~/.pi/agent/models.json; rejected to avoid clobbering user-managed Pi configuration.

acceptance_criteria.Pi appears as primary harness option: Implemented via _ALL_HARNESSES, CLI choices, picker, aliases.
acceptance_criteria.Detection and install hint: Implemented via core.machine_profile command_version('pi') and Pi install hint pointing to pi.dev/npm package.
acceptance_criteria.Wizard run completes smoke and verify: Implemented via _wire_pi, step_2_7_verify, _build_oneshot_cmd, helper script support; covered by unit and e2e-style test infrastructure.
acceptance_criteria.guide.md launch docs: Guide template documents PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR, ccl provider, model flags.
acceptance_criteria.Pi docs/install hint references: README and install hints reference https://pi.dev and model-agnostic positioning.
